已知你遊各種描述放飛自我，希望可以追加明確屬性標籤。

尤其是阿拉及沙耶一類，新界域描述還是xx傷害%沒進行調整，沒測過還真不知道那兩個是大招終傷跟指令卡終傷。

一方面讓玩家需要自行實測，另一方面製作組也開始出錯了，普希跟莉莉終傷互乘bug就是其中之一，24原本也是受害者，你們願意回調24倒還好，某些角色不回調就很打擊玩家熱情，最好方法就是盡量避免搞出相同情況。

具體想法是通過一些底層代碼，讀取該技能、天賦或界域系統實際算式，並為算式內的各種buff額外追加提供一個明確屬性名字，像是「銀充」、「銀充%」、「狂氣%」、「狂氣返還」、「狂氣獲取(如安息之吻那類)」、「基礎傷害」、「狂氣爆發基礎傷害」、「指令卡基礎傷害」、「終傷」、「狂氣爆發終傷」、「指令卡終傷」、「暴擊率」、「暴傷」等等，再通過標籤方式顯示出來具體buff名字，與代碼算式那個指定屬性掛鉤，所以顯示出來的結果必定不會出錯。

假設皮克曼大招、命輪及靈塑都可以看到標籤「狂氣爆發終傷」，然後沙耶繁血界域描述也可以看到標籤「狂氣爆發終傷」，那麼兩者buff是否同類、是否可以互乘，表達方面一目了然。

同時也避免了出bug，哪怕出bug也容易抓到。

當然除非當初標籤名字就寫錯了，那我也無話可說。

This can probably be chalked up to skill issue on my part, but I didn't discover that Exalts could be Upgraded (in the Skills tab) until recently - lvl 46 after a couple of weeks. I'm not sure if it was ever stated in the intro tutorial hand-holding, but I usually fast-forward through that and don't remember it all anyways.
If it could happen to me, it could happen to many other players too. I always thought the Text-bubble for the Exalt was just informative and distinct from the Cards below which are more obviously upgradeable. Maybe there's a way to make it more intuitive, either visually re-shifting things or adding a hand-holding tutorial "click here" at a slightly later point in the tutorial phase.

Im here to complain about the "Sabotage" mechanic from arc 2 chapter 5 but ESPECIALLY this weeks wave 3 Dtide boss. 

The mechanic itself is completely anti fun, taking this much damage when trying to just play the game and pull off your combos is completely unreasonable.

This weeks Dtide boss is especially horrible because you cant remove sabotage, if you play your card and redraw it back the sabotage is still present and you essentially take double damage without being able to do anything? What exactly is the players counterplay to the enemies doing this to your cards? 

Some of the most frustrating and unrewarding game design choices i had to encounter in this game. Atleast remove the sabotage after the card has been played...
am i just supposed to ignore my main damage card or?

I agree. There are levels to difficulty in this game. You can't counter this and the mechanic is punishing you (and not a small punishment mind you) for playing cards in a card game. And the enemies that apply it aren't a pushover they hit very hard too

Hello Developer,

I am a Korean player who always enjoys playing the game.

I am contacting you with a suggestion regarding the Wheel of Destiny. Specifically, I would like to propose a buff for Max Breakthrough Wheels of Destiny.

At the moment, the only significant advantage of a Max Breakthrough Wheel of Destiny seems to be the ability to equip additional Wheels of Destiny. Other than that, it does not feel particularly rewarding or valuable.

Therefore, I would like to suggest the following improvements:

Lower the requirements or thresholds for equipping additional Wheels of Destiny.
Add extra effects that are specifically tied to the character released alongside that Wheel of Destiny.
If the associated character equips the Wheel of Destiny, the additional effect would be applied at 100% effectiveness.
If any other character equips it, the additional effect would still apply, but at 50% effectiveness.

I believe these changes would make Max Breakthrough Wheels of Destiny feel more meaningful and worthwhile, while also strengthening the connection between specific characters and their signature Wheels of Destiny.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Hello,<@1470253170209853632>

I am a dedicated player who has been thoroughly enjoying Morimens for about six months. I am writing this message to bring to your attention some critical issues regarding the current Korean localization in the game.

Despite the involvement of an official Korean translator, major translation issues—specifically regarding consistent quality control and glossary management—remain unresolved. Below are some of the key issues that Korean players have frequently encountered during gameplay.

1. Lack of Consistency in In-Game Translation
There is a noticeable lack of consistency across character names, skill names, skill descriptions, and world-building proper nouns.

Proper Nouns & Lore: Names of characters and lore-specific terms within the story are translated inconsistently. In some cases, they differ significantly from the original source text, which severely disrupts immersion in the game's otherwise wonderful world and storyline (e.g., the inconsistent translation of "Misisk University").

Skill Names & Descriptions: There are frequent discrepancies where a skill name differs from how it is referenced inside the skill's actual description text. Furthermore, frequent changes to skill names (most notably for Murphy: Fauxborn and Kathigu-Ra) cause heavy confusion. Changing established skill names unfamiliarizes players with their characters and degrades the overall gameplay experience.

2. Inconsistent Honorifics and Speech Tones (Polite vs. Casual Speech)
Throughout the story, characters frequently switch between polite formal speech (Jondetmal) and casual informal speech (Banmal) without any logical reason.

An immersive storytelling experience is abruptly broken when a character who was just speaking formally suddenly switches to casual speech, or vice versa. In subculture games, a character's tone and manner of speech define their personality and lore. This inconsistency completely shatters character identity and collapses established character settings (most notably seen in the dialogue of Murphy and Murphy: Fauxborn).

These ongoing errors serve as a clear indicator that minimal glossary management and In-Game LQA (Localization Quality Assurance) are not being properly conducted, even after the onboarding of an official translator.

Since this issue can ultimately damage the trust between the player base and the development team, we sincerely hope you will listen to our feedback. As players who deeply love and enjoy this game, we kindly request more meticulous management to help the world-building and gameplay of Morimens truly shine.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Best regards,
A devoted Morimens player

특정 단어 통일 문제

한국에서는 중요한 문제인 존댓말 < > 반말을 왔다 갔다 하는 문제

이것도 단어 통일 문제지만 수호자 각성체의 이름이 왔다 갔다 하는 문제
비밀 지킴이, 수호자, 웨이크업 바디 등

한국 정서에 맞지 않는 번역  
예) Solar Flare = 태양 플레어
이건 태양 플레어가 틀린 번역도 아니고 오히려 맞는 번역이지만
게임과 그리고 한국인의 정서 읽히는 단어 발음 등등

게임을 좀 해본 사람이거나 한국인이라면 태양 플레어가 아닌
솔라 플레어로 번역했을 겁니다.

이런 문제들 그리고 단순하게 단어 통일 문제는
코딩이 어떻게 되어 있는지 정확하게 저 같은 문외한은 모르지만

" " 안에 단어만 찾아서 바꾸면 되는 작업이면
이렇게 오래 걸릴 문제인지 솔직히 의문이 있습니다.
필요하다면 한국 커뮤니티 같은 곳에서 공유해서
오역을 유저들한테 찾아 달라고 부탁하는 방법도 있습니다.
분명히 금방 해결할 수 있는 문제입니다.

그리고 한국어 번역 담당자인 젤리님한테 직접 소통해도 되는지
소통할 생각은 있는지도 궁금합니다.

content: 댓글로 남겨야겠네용

Hello,

I am a Korean player who enjoys playing Forgetting Eve, and I have a great deal to say regarding the current translation quality.

After reviewing the Korean localization, I have come to believe that the official translator has not been providing translations with sufficient care and consistency.

Due to the recent event, many players have been spending a significant amount of money to support the game. Some players even spend as much as 500,000 KRW in a single day. However, many users have raised concerns after seeing the current state of the translation, and I have also noticed numerous issues myself.

In Korean, there is a clear distinction between formal speech and informal speech. The current translation often fails to distinguish between the two, resulting in unnatural dialogue and characterization.
User-translated content and GPT-generated (official) translations appear to be mixed together, creating inconsistency throughout the game.
Many of the terms used in the game's worldbuilding differ from what Korean-speaking players understand them to be after reviewing and translating the original content ourselves.

These issues reflect a lack of attention and quality control in the localization process.

I sincerely ask that you take the time to read this message and consider addressing these problems. We care deeply about the game and want its Korean localization to meet the same standard as the rest of the experience.


Sincerely,
A player who loves Morimens.

When purchasing the "Extra Curriculum" (Battlepass) late into the game version, the game promises that I will receive retroactive Reality Verges for all the days I've logged in. HOWEVER, due to the Reality Verges being capped at 10, I cannot receive all these promised rewards. This is very poor user experience, especially for a pack I pay for. 

Please uncap Reality Verges so that we can accrue as many as we like. There is no reason to force a limit of 10 when there are so many stories now available. This arbitrary limit is an outdated feature that should be lifted.
